llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm (copilot) vs. one control arm (no AI). Copilot arm selected as the human+AI condition.
llm_extracted.notes: After thorough re-read of the entire paper, no additional statistical information was found beyond what was previously extracted. The paper reports only means and percentage improvements for the primary outcome (accuracy_all_content). No standard deviations, standard errors, t-statistics, F-statistics, or standardized effect sizes (Cohen's d or Hedges' g) are provided anywhere in the document for the overall accuracy comparison. P-values are consistently reported only as inequalities (p < .001, indicated by *** notation) rather than exact values. Table 2 on page 6 shows the means (Treatment: 6.97, Control: 5.18) but no SDs or SEs. The methodology section describes the experimental design and grading but does not report dispersion statistics. The results section (pages 5-6) focuses on percentage improvements and significance levels but never provides the underlying variance measures needed for effect size calculation. This remains a Level F extraction (means + n + p-value inequality only), which is insufficient for computing a reliable Hedges' g. The paper appears to have been designed to report percentage improvements rather than standardized effect sizes.
source_quotes: Across all scenarios and tasks, Copilot subjects experienced a 34.53% improvement in overall accuracy and a 29.79% reduction in task completion time.
Table 2: Copilot's effect on accuracy by question type and scenario. Aggregate Results All Content: Possible Points 11, Treatment 6.97, Control 5.18, Improvement 34.53%***
We had 181 subjects complete the task satisfactorily.
Half of the subjects were assigned to the control group and were granted access to the Entra and Intune admin centers. The other half were assigned to the treatment group
